blocks.title: Ecological Pyramids
points: The food or energy relationship among organisms at different trophic levels can be expressed in terms of number, biomass or energy.
The expression assumes the shape of a pyramid where the base represents producers and the apex represents tertiary consumers.
Each trophic level represents a functional level and not any one species in particular.

blocks.title: Definition
blocks.content: Ecological pyramids are graphical representations of trophic structure or food chains showing organisms at different trophic levels.

blocks.title: Pyramid of Number in Grassland Ecosystem
blocks.caption: Only three top carnivores are supported by nearly six million plants.

blocks.title: Gray Matter Alert
blocks.content: The term ecological pyramid was given by Charles Elton. The concept of pyramid of numbers was developed by him.

blocks.title: Previous Year's Question
blocks.question: The pyramid of energy is always

points: In general all ecological pyramids are upright.
Pyramid of energy is always upright because energy is lost as heat at each trophic level.
